summary: Career Explorer V2 first-finish smoke test — all backend API contracts (countries, careers list/detail with country multipliers, auth full flow incl. forgot/reset, assessments CRUD, saved-careers idempotent, admin/stats RBAC, AI explain via Claude Sonnet 4.5) verified end-to-end against the public URL. Frontend smoke covered landing, signup -> dashboard, authed header (logout + country-select), empty-state, career detail with 3-stage roadmap, /admin RBAC redirect for non-admin, and admin dashboard counters + top careers for admin login. Everything is green.
passed: GET /api/careers?country=IN -> 5 careers, symbol ₹, salary scaled by 38x (e.g. 2,660,000 – 6,080,000)
GET /api/careers/Software%20Engineer?country=UK -> full detail, salary in £ (£49,000 – £112,000), roadmap has beginner/intermediate/advanced
POST /api/auth/register -> 200 {token,user}; GET /api/auth/me with Bearer -> 200 (no password_hash); /me without token -> 401
Duplicate register -> 409; wrong password login -> 401; correct login -> 200
POST /api/auth/reset-password (uses field name 'password') -> 200; subsequent login with new password -> 200
Assessments: POST -> 200 id; GET -> list of 1; DELETE -> 200; unauthenticated GET -> 401
Saved careers: POST -> 200, second POST idempotent -> 200, GET list -> 1, DELETE -> 200
Admin login (admin@careerexplorer.ai / Admin@2026!) -> 200; GET /api/admin/stats -> 200 with total_users/total_assessments/total_saved/top_careers/top_saved; non-admin -> 403
POST /api/ai/explain (Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations) -> 200 with non-empty explanation; unauth -> 401
FRONTEND signup form (signup-name/email/password/country/submit) signs in user and redirects to /dashboard; header now shows logout-button + country-select
FRONTEND dashboard shows no-assessments empty state for a fresh user
FRONTEND /admin redirects non-admin to / (admin-title not rendered)
FRONTEND admin login renders admin-title + counter-users + counter-assessments + counter-saved-careers
minor_notes: Reset-password endpoint expects JSON field 'password' (not 'new_password'); worked correctly with 'password'. Frontend ResetPasswordPage already uses 'password' so this is fine, but worth documenting.
